action.reasoning: Observation: The current best balanced_accuracy is 0.8514 from iteration 9 (ConvNeXt-Tiny, seed 42). Iteration 14 trained a second ConvNeXt with seed 123 (convnext_s2.pt) and created a weight-soup that scored 0.8462, slightly below the best. A logit-level ensemble of the two raw ConvNeXt models should outperform the weight-soup because it preserves model diversity and allows per-sample arbitration. This requires zero training time. Analysis: Since both models share the same preprocessing, I can average their logits on the validation set, grid-search the interpolation weight and post-hoc logit-adjustment temperature, and save a joint ensemble checkpoint. I must update predict.py to load ensemble checkpoints containing multiple model state dicts. Decision: Update ensemble_tune.py and predict.py for robust multi-model ensemble support, then immediately run ensemble tuning on best_iter9.pt + convnext_s2.pt, smoke-test, and submit eval. If this doesn't beat 0.8514, the next iteration will train a ResNet50 for architectural diversity.
action.command: python ensemble_tune.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/convnext_s2.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_cnext.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_cnext.pt --output /tmp/ens_test.json && echo "Smoke test OK, preds:" $(python -c "import json; print(len(json.load(open('/tmp/ens_test.json'))['predictions']))")
